RT @curtis_yarvin: There’s no reason a software company needs a mailing address

Maybe this will get some hate

But my heart goes out fully to all users affected

Not Drift

There are thousands of users who lost their life savings

Yes, it was their decision to use Drift, but Drift marketed themselves as the best place to trade, to park stables, etc.

And Drift disappointed (some would say betrayed) users

Not by falling for social engineering but for handling weak opsec while managing over $500M

And yes, I can't imagine how bad the Drift team feels, but the truth is life continues for them

They had huge paychecks, will get hired by other companies, etc.

The past proved this

With higher payment comes higher responsibility and accountability, which is currently totally missing from the Drift team

Maybe that's a legal issue and still sympathy doesn't cost you anything

We've had Bybit pushing announcements every minute, doing livestreams, etc.

We have Drift and it's radio silent

Weekly Update – @NOMADZxyz | Week 15 [2026]  

1.1 Quick wins: 
1. Built 10 new Essential badges and a new revenue share program 
2. Tested and fixed issues with aggregator v.2 
3. Shipped discount codes for Nomadz 100 and Accelerate
4. Had a launch campaign with @the_mindfolk and @cherrydotfun 
5. Crossed 17.6K registered users, with 150 DAU and 28.57% retention rate

1.2 What's next: 
1. Platform: launch referral program with tiers, add @superteam events to calendar, update payment flow to @moonpay, integrate a new auth system
2. Mobile: Launch v. 11 with new quests, badges and events 
3. Partnership campaign with a secret community (not signed yet)

2. Highlights  
2.1 Product - Platform 
1. Improved aggregator with v.2 is tested and issues are fixed
2. Discount codes for Nomadz 100, @SolanaEvents and @NFCsummit are shipped
3. 10 new Essential badges and a new revenue share program

2.2 Product - @solanamobile
  
1. Communities verification, new badges and quests, new events will be live this week

2.3 Growth
1. Launched podcasts with @theindexshow and @the_mindfolk 
2. Launched a campaign with @cherrydotfun and @perena 
 3. Sent the forth email campaign: 15,000 emails delivered. 
4. Crossed 17,600+ registered users and 20,000+ followers on X. 
5. Onboarded 19 new creators to Nomadz 100 and hosted second community call.

3. Lowlights  
1. On the final stage for signing major partnerships
2. Have not been able to secure more events partnerships

4. Core Metrics  
1. Total Users: 17,654 (↑ from 17,303 last week) 
2. Active Users (week): 1047 (↑ from 787 last week) 
3. New Users (week):  329 (↑ from 172 last week) 
4. DAU: ~149 on average (↑ from 113 last week) 
5. Transactions on Solana (week): ~2019 (↑ from 6420 last week) 
6. Retention: 28.57% (↑ from 28.02% last week)
6. X Followers (@NOMADZxyz): 20,005k+ (↑ from 19,915)  

5. This Week's Focus  
→ Launch discount codes for communities
→ Events calendar with Superteam events 
→ Launch Nomadz 100 Cohort 2 and have the second community call 
→ Sign new BIG partnership and do a launch campaign
 
 6. Asks  
1. Looking for companies actively traveling to help them save on hotels and provide concierge service  

7. Shoutouts 
Big thanks to the @solana team for providing fresh ideas and support to us. Also, for connecting with relevant people. Grateful for the hard work of my dev and growth teams despite. 

It was a hard, but productive week. We will ship more this week!

A) The semi truck in image A would need to have been moving at approximately 55 mph (89 km/h or 24.6 m/s) when the hard braking began.

Not B because: the concave sloshing is due to a turn that occurred recently at no more than 20mph.  There is no way for the truck to have picked up enough speed to get to 55mph 

Not C because: the dramatic rear pile-up and forward-crested wave in image C can only realistically occur during the early, initial stage of strong acceleration from a relatively low speed.

but I thought of a more accurate phrase for alternate finance:

cypher-capitalism

property rights, free trade, and the rule of contracts that capitalism requires, enforced by cryptography

note that this is for relatively complex systems and backend work 

not referring to basic UI stuff, vibecoding randomly, or using it as a search engine

🚨BREAKING: @Solana Foundation launched STRIDE, alongside 24/7 threat monitoring and a new crisis response network for Solana DeFi following the Drift hack.

➡️Protocols above $10M TVL that pass STRIDE will get 24/7 threat monitoring.

➡️Protocols above $100M TVL will also receive Foundation funded formal verification.

➡️Solana Foundation also launched SIRN, a new incident response network led by Asymmetric, OtterSec, Neodyme, Squads, and ZeroShadow.

you're in crypto and holding $50k+ onchain? 
make sure to: 

> have at least 2 hardware wallets from different manufacturers
> dedicated device for crypto only. no browsing, no apps, no email 
> seed phrases stored in 2 separate physical locations
> cold wallet for holding. hot wallet for interacting. never mix the two
> add a 25th word passphrase to your hardware wallet. seed alone is not enough
> always on VPN on every device you use for crypto
> separate email exclusively for crypto
> no cloud backup. ever. iCloud, Google Drive are not your friends
> hardware wallets stored in a safe. not on your desk
> limit your onchain interactions. every dapp is an exposure point
> never post wallet addresses or portfolio sizes publicly
> every 3 months, review all approvals, connected apps, and wallet activity
> keep your crypto identity and your real identity fully separate

most people with serious size are still operating like they have nothing to lose.

please do it, now! not later.

The war against Iran, a timeline:

Mar 3: “We won the war.”

Mar 7: “We defeated Iran.”

Mar 9: “We must attack Iran.” “The war is ending almost completely, and very beautifully.”

Mar 12: “We did win, but we haven’t won completely yet.”

Mar 13: “We won the war.”

Mar 14: “Please help us.”

Mar 15: “If you don’t help us, I will certainly remember it.”

Mar 16: “Actually, we don’t need any help at all.” “I was just testing to see who’s listening to me.” “If NATO doesn’t help, they will suffer something very bad.”

Mar 17: “We neither need nor want NATO’s help.” “I don’t need Congressional approval to withdraw from NATO.”

Mar 18: “Our allies must cooperate in reopening the Strait of Hormuz.”

Mar 19: “US allies need to get a grip -step up and help open the Strait of Hormuz”

Mar 20: “NATO are cowards.”

Mar 21: “We don’t use it, we don’t need to open it.”

Mar 22: “This is the last time. I will give Iran 48 hours.” “Iran is Dead”

Mar 23: “We are giving them more time.”

Mar 24: “The war is nearing its end.”

Mar 25: “We are still negotiating.”

Mar 26: “Iran is begging for peace. They gave us a gift. We will give them more time.”

Mar 27: “Talks with iran are going very well”

Mar 28: “War will be over soon”

Mar 29: “Maybe we take Kharg island, maybe we dont”

Mar 30: “Open the Strait or we will obliterate all energy infrastructure and oil wells”

Mar 31: “We dont need the strait, we got plenty of oil. Get it yourself UK.”

Apr 1: “War will be over in two to three weeks”

Apr 4: “Iran has 48 hours or I’ll reign down hell”

Apr 5: “Praise be to Allah! Open the fucking straight!”

You cannot make this up.

What a fucking joke lmao.

RT @PapiChuloGrim: You mean I have to start asking people at conferences to say “Fuck Kim Jong Un” as well now???

Sadly I think it’s gonna take at least a few more of these before everyone takes it seriously enough to stop putting it off.

Drift is the first one that felt like it hit home for the entire industry all at once, especially due to the insane sophistication of the attack.

These state actors could be your friends, your bd partners, your employees and you would never even know.

I’m guilty as anyone when it comes to “that would never happen to me”. But in reality it could happen to any one of us, at any time, and we wouldn’t find out until it was too late.

Large crypto protocols hold 100s of millions and even billions of dollars. Secured by code that could have bugs, and humans that are vulnerable for many other reasons. 

There are state actors right now that have one singular job that they are training for, all day every day. The job is to take your money by any means necessary, and everyone is a target.

I would go as far as saying that every financial  protocol should go back through every single hire in your organization, every bd relationship you have and make sure you have a logical trail of validation and endorsements from others you trust. If you don’t have a chain of trust, would remain skeptical of that person or group. 

Maybe it sounds crazy, but ultimately it’s your business and your users that will be hurt the most.

Going to a psychologist is a perfect example of something that has become normalized, expensive, and rarely questioned.

A lot of Americans will hate me for this one because many of you deeply rely on services like this.

The United States spends more on healthcare than any country on earth. Mental health spending alone in the U.S. exceeds $280 billion annually, and therapy sessions often cost $100 to $250+ per hour without insurance.

Why is it so high?

- Therapy is culturally normalized
- The healthcare system is largely private
- Higher prices per visit
- More services being consumed

High spending does not automatically mean better outcomes. It often just means higher prices lol and more dependence on the system.

If therapy truly helps you, keep going. Nobody is saying you should stop.

Most modern systems are designed around management, not resolution.

Long-term problems create long-term revenue. That is not a conspiracy. That is economics.

Most people search outside for answers that were always inside.

Stress, anxiety, depression, and burnout are strongly linked to lifestyle factors like sleep, nutrition, movement, sunlight, relationships, and purpose. These are not random magical forces, they are clear signals from your body and mind.

When life makes you uncomfortable, sick, or mentally overwhelmed, it is not always punishment. It is feedback. Sometimes it is awareness knocking on the door. Your body, mind, and soul are asking you to wake up my friend.

The modern world teaches you, "I have the solution. You don’t. Come back next week”.

Spiritual ancient traditions have always taught the opposite: Know yourself. Observe yourself. Heal yourself through awareness. Question the problem. Question the habits behind it. Question the thoughts that repeat inside your mind.

Sit in silence. Feel your breath. Meditate. Pay attention to your body. Become conscious of who you truly are.

That is where true healing begins.

There is no need to go to extremes. I'm not telling you to reject doctors or avoid hospitals. Use medicine when necessary.

Just remember that you are not powerless. You are not broken. You are not just a customer in this corrupt system. You already have the answers within you. It’s time people remember who they are and stop relying so much on external band aids.

You are almost always the root of the problem and the root of the solution.

Often during conversations with founders in Dubai I say: “We have already gone through something similar in Ukraine in 2022”. 

The main lesson that we have learned since then is simple, but at the same time fundamental: you cannot stop.

Because a product will not produce itself, market itself, or sell itself. That is why the key formula for survival and success during a crisis is constant movement forward: to work, adapt, and look for new opportunities. 

The experience of Ukrainian businesses has shown that even in the most difficult circumstances, those who continue to act have a chance not only to survive, but also to thrive.

You cannot stop!

🚨 @DriftProtocol hack update
The team confirms this was a 6-month coordinated social engineering campaign, not a smart contract exploit—attackers posed as a trading firm, built trust across IRL meetings + onchain activity, then compromised contributors via malicious repos & fake wallet apps.

Drift has fully frozen the protocol, removed compromised wallets from multisig, and is working with security firms & law enforcement while publicly sharing details to help the ecosystem learn—urging all Solana teams to treat every multisig device as a target and audit access immediately.

The attack is linked with medium-high confidence to Lazarus Group–style operations, reinforcing that human-layer security is now DeFi’s biggest risk vector. ⚠️

👉 Anatoly Yakovenko noted Drift handled loss socialization fairly, suggesting a path forward: rebuild with a lean core team + compensate users via structured recovery (e.g. airdrops).

⚠️ Takeaway: this isn’t just a Drift incident—it’s a wake-up call for all Solana projects to harden opsec, isolate signer devices, and rethink trust in offchain interactions.
